text:
Kenya's 20-year-old Olympic champion Emmanuel Wanyonyi delivered a standout performance at the Wanda Diamond League meeting in Lausanne on Thursday, where he clocked a blistering 1:41.11 in the men's 800m.This remarkable time catapulted Wanyonyi to joint second on the world all-time list, just 0.20 seconds shy of the world record of 1:40.91 set by his fellow Kenyan and role model, David Rudisha, at the London 2012 Olympics.Get Full Access for Ksh299/WeekUncover the stories others won’t tell. title: Diamond League: Olympic champion Wanyonyi moves to second on world all-time 800m list
author: Washington Onyango
description: Wanyonyi clocked a world leading time of 1:41.11 in Lausanne.
